critic.verdict: An exceptionally well-flowing Easy chart that perfectly captures the vocal rhythms and has flawless physical flow.

issues.problem: Hold-to-hold transitions on off-beats (half-beats) might be slightly challenging for true beginners on an Easy chart, though they fit the synth swells well.
A continuous stream of off-beat quarter notes followed by an eighth-note transition can be tricky for the target difficulty, but it matches the vocal syncopation perfectly.

critic.verdict: The chart suffers from extreme repetition, relying almost exclusively on Left-Right jumps (JUMP LR) for every single jump sequence, resulting in awkward footwork and a lack of creative pattern variety.

issues.problem: Extreme overuse of 'JUMP LR'. Virtually every jump in the chorus is a Left-Right jump, completely ignoring other jump variations (like UD, LD, RD, etc.), making the chart feel incredibly one-dimensional and repetitive.

The transition 'tap L' followed immediately by 'JUMP LR' 0.25 seconds later creates an uncomfortable double-tap on the left foot while trying to execute a jump, which is too jerky for a Medium difficulty chart.
The repetitive loop of JUMP LR -> tap U -> tap D -> tap L -> JUMP LR forces highly repetitive and awkward foot placement, lacking natural alternating flow.

critic.verdict: An exceptionally energetic and well-timed chart with a fantastic drop, though marred by a few highly awkward cross-over streams in the first half.

issues.problem: The repeating 'D -> R -> L -> U' pattern forces uncomfortable double cross-overs (Left foot on R, Right foot on L), causing awkward body twists.
Transitions like 'R -> L -> D' require extreme leg crossing that breaks the flow of the stream and is difficult to execute comfortably.
